skills.frame-analogize: Build and stress-test an analogy for a settled concept. Use for ELI5, metaphors, or 'explain differently'; compares candidates and names where the winner breaks.
skills.frame-dialectic: Put a forming claim on trial: steelman both sides, expose its load-bearing assumption, and name a deciding experiment. Use for devil's advocacy or thesis stress tests.
skills.frame-first-principles: Rebuild a question or decision from irreducible axioms, separating necessity from convention. Use to challenge assumptions or reason from first principles.
skills.frame-graft: Map every primitive of a mature donor model onto a new problem, classifying fit, break, and adapt. Use to design through another system's structure.
skills.frame-orthogonal: Factor a tangled problem into independent axes and verify that moving one leaves the others fixed. Use to expose conflated or falsely coupled dimensions.
skills.nav-audit: Audit codebase architecture against deep-module principles. Use for health, smells, feature-readiness, or full-domain reviews; read-only.
skills.nav-compose: Author or restructure prose as a navigable deep module: lead with the point, group by knowledge, and keep each fact owned once.
skills.nav-do: Implement a small, decided behavior change without a plan artifact. Use for quick features or fixes, not refactors or ambiguous work.
skills.nav-map: Render or refresh a bilingual repo map from load-bearing files and headers. Use for architecture maps; use nav-tour for conversational onboarding.
skills.nav-plan: Turn a feature intent into a codebase-grounded implementation plan after auditing touched domains and resolving ambiguity. Writes only the approved plan.
skills.nav-refactor: Execute a behavior-preserving structural refactor with verbatim moves and test gates. Use to extract, split, or reorganize code without changing behavior.
skills.nav-sync: Refresh load-bearing files' top headers to describe their purpose, details, and dependencies after code changes.
skills.nav-tour: Guide a read-only conversation through what a codebase does, how, and why; labels rationale Recorded/Inferred/Unknown and ends with a model for the user to correct.
skills.reflect-catchup: Rebuild current work state from HANDOFF.md, git, files, plans, and live context. Use after a break; read-only and present-state only.
skills.reflect-observe: Surface durable learning candidates, then write only the user's selected observations or scrubbed skill feedback.
skills.reflect-park: Write goal, done, now, open, next, and the current SHA to HANDOFF.md before stepping away. Explicitly invoked; overwrites the prior cursor.
skills.reflect-retrace: Retrace why development changed stage by stage, get user correction, then render an evidence-backed interactive artifact. Not catchup or tour.
skills.relay-brief: Maintain a cited Brief from active Decision files when understanding must stay current across contexts. Always a pull request; never closes work, claims consensus, or changes Core.
skills.relay-digest: Show the viewer's GitHub obligations: assigned work, reviews/re-reviews, settlement, and requesting a reviewer for an unreviewed PR. Read-only; mentions are notices.
skills.relay-launch: Set up or audit Relay in the current GitHub repository: identity, Discussions, permissions, CODEOWNERS, and protection. Writes require approval and read-back.
skills.relay-migrate: Migrate pre-model coordination state into Accord memory while preserving provenance. Explicitly invoked, mapping-first, idempotent, and write-gated.
skills.relay-reply: Respond on an existing GitHub object with input, acceptance, an answer, comment, PR Comment, Approve, or Request changes. Never closes the object.
skills.relay-report: Route a tell, owned question, review request, or memory change under the Issue-default Accord model. Previews writes and verifies responsibility.
skills.relay-settle: Settle a Discussion or Issue, record a promoted Decision, merge an approved PR, or abandon one with reason. Requires authority and preserves Core gates.
skills.research-critique: Adversarially review an argument document: test claims against evidence, locate real sources of gains, and self-attack criticisms. Use for referee or evidence reviews.
skills.research-dissect: Break an argument document into Gap, Claim, Mechanism, Evidence, and Conclusion; also check cited-as against actually-says for individual citations.
skills.research-provenance: Audit citations in your document by tracing load-bearing claims, numbers, and quotes to verified sources; classify first-hand, second-hand, or orphaned.
skills.research-untangle: Map lineage, clusters, contradictions, and contested ground across argument documents. Use for literature landscapes, related work, or locating a claim.
skills.shape-align: Decide with the user what is In progress, Next, and Future, then maintain a lean plan.md board. Use to align priorities or refresh what comes next.
skills.shape-build: Drive a blueprints plan's In-progress items to verified completion with scoped execution, checks, and board updates.
skills.shape-dogfood: Use a completed feature like a real user via browser or CLI, record evidence, and identify workflow friction or missing intent coverage.
skills.shape-elicit: Grill the user to a conceptual decision or root cause. Use for undecided logic, not visuals; may offer shape-survey or shape-probe when stalled.
skills.shape-mockup: Converge a visual, structural, flow, or option decision through an interactive HTML artifact. Use to mock, compare, diagram, or try variants.
skills.shape-position: Author and maintain a project's ratified core principles from messy inputs. Use to establish positioning, graduate durable thoughts, or repair canon.
skills.shape-probe: Design and run a minimal experiment when argument cannot settle a fork. Use to test an undecided claim, not preference or workflow friction.
skills.shape-reconcile: Compare blueprints thoughts, plans, and mockups with shipped reality, then propose per-file amend, consolidate, graduate, retire, or prune actions. Read-only until approved.
skills.shape-setup: Scaffold a new project to a verified running baseline from an archetype or grounded exemplar. Use to start projects, not add features.
skills.shape-survey: Map an unfamiliar repo-grounded decision space into independent axes, then show what differs from the user's current model. Not for repo maps or research.
